conversations.content:
> Regarding your earlier message:
> Got it — you're asking about the MCP server portion that runs inside Claude.ai (claude.ai/code or similar), not the web app itself.

and???

No MCP server code has been built in this project. What exists is a web app (Express backend + React frontend) for visually building and running agent workflows. The MCP server component — the piece that would let Claude Code or claude.ai interact with this system as a tool — has not been implemented.

If you want to add that, it would be a new sprint: build an MCP server that exposes the workflow runner as tools Claude can call. Want me to scope that out?

Ok here is an example to work with and the idea is that the mcp app would connect to this app to kickoff flows and check status of runs. maybe it can even create flows as an option. 

The user attached a large paste (Code, 7465 characters, 264 lines). The full content has been saved to a file — read it before responding.

File: /Users/raminhedayatpour/Documents/VibeProjects/ramin-hedayatpour-BFP27fWc/managed-agents-poc-8gnj/.vibing-assets/long-messages/user-input-1776869481641.txt

Preview (first 5 lines):
# Flow Manager MCP App for Claude Code

## Overview
This MCP app lets Claude Code **trigger workflow runs** and **check status** against your flow manager backend. Users can say "Run my content pipeline with topic X" or "What's the status of run abc123".

... (264 total lines)

Sprint 11 is created with 5 tasks:

1. Scaffold the `mcp/` package with its own `package.json` and MCP SDK dependency
2. Add `GET /api/workflows` to the backend if it doesn't exist
3. Core tools: `list_workflows`, `start_workflow`, `get_run_status`
4. Additional tools: `list_runs` and `create_workflow`
5. README with the exact `claude mcp add` command and usage examples

Once executed, you'll register it in Claude Code with a single command and be able to say things like "run my content pipeline workflow with topic X" or "what's the status of run abc123" directly in chat. Want me to kick it off now?
The deployed is saying it needs my permission to write a file. can you help it

The Coder agent is paused and needs you to approve a file write. Look for the permission prompt in the active task panel and click Allow — it's trying to modify `mcp/src/client.ts` to fix the event field name bug from Sprint 11's code review.

I approve

Attached files (1):
1. /Users/raminhedayatpour/Documents/VibeProjects/ramin-hedayatpour-BFP27fWc/managed-agents-poc-8gnj/.vibing-assets/attachments/attachment-1776871534535-whc023pun.png

The site is live and fully working. That error you saw was from the first failed attempt — it's been superseded by the successful deployment.

**Live URL:** https://managed-agents-poc-8gnj.vercel.app

The Workflow Workbench is loading correctly with the visual editor, node palette, and all tabs (Editor, Run History, Chat). Note that API calls to the backend (saving/loading workflows, running agents) will not work on this URL since the Express + SQLite backend isn't deployed — the frontend is static only on Vercel.

Attached files (2):
1. /Users/raminhedayatpour/Documents/VibeProjects/ramin-hedayatpour-BFP27fWc/managed-agents-poc-8gnj/.vibing-assets/attachments/attachment-1776871602824-iotagabxm.png
2. /Users/raminhedayatpour/Documents/VibeProjects/ramin-hedayatpour-BFP27fWc/managed-agents-poc-8gnj/.vibing-assets/attachments/attachment-1776871602916-j7wdpxltd.png

Expected behavior — and the core limitation of deploying only the frontend to Vercel. Both errors are HTTP 404 because the React app is calling `/api/runs` and `/api/chat` (your Express backend), which doesn't exist at that URL on Vercel.

The backend (Express + SQLite) needs to be deployed separately for the app to be fully functional. **Railway** would be the simplest option — it can host the Node.js server with the SQLite database in a single service. Want me to set that up?
arg!. Ok. maybe we move everything to render?
Task completed
